body:
Closes #5593.

One-line fix to `BatchedTridiagonalSolver`: size scratch by `worksize(grid)` rather than `(grid.Nx, grid.Ny, grid.Nz)`. For non-RFTRG grids `worksize(grid) == size(grid)`, so this is a strict no-op everywhere except `RightFaceFolded` tripolar grids — where the existing `worksize` override returns `(Nx, Ny+1, Nz)` and the kernel was writing `t[i, Ny+1, k]` out of bounds.

See #5593 for the full root-cause walkthrough, a 30-line `--check-bounds=yes` reproducer that throws `BoundsError: attempt to access 8×8×4 Array{Float64, 3} at index [1, 9, 2]`, and the two-option fix discussion. This PR implements Option 1 (the surgical one).

Verified locally with `--check-bounds=yes`:
- main: `BoundsError` at `[1, 9, 2]` in scratch sized `(8, 8, 4)`, thrown from `solve_batched_tridiagonal_system_z!`
- this branch: runs clean, no error

No regression test in this PR — see the "Recommended added test" section of #5593 for the suggested follow-up (add an implicit-V-diffusion closure to existing tripolar fixtures like `test_zstar_conservation_tripolar.jl` so `Pkg.test()`'s default `--check-bounds=yes` would have caught this).

labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

title: Size BatchedTridiagonalSolver scratch by worksize, fixing OOB on RightFaceFolded tripolar grids
